hook.text: Instead of 'build referrals,' hypothesis-driven teams say: 'Referrals will boost signups 15% because users trust peers.'

buildup.text: A hypothesis has three parts: what we believe (the change), what will happen (the metric impact), and why (the reasoning).

twist.text: The power of hypotheses is that they can be wrong — and that's okay. A disproven hypothesis is still a successful experiment.

climax.text: Hypothesis-driven teams learn faster because every feature ships with a prediction.

quiz.question: What are the three parts of a product hypothesis?
options: What we believe (the change), what will happen (the metric impact), and why (the reasoning)
Who will use it, how much it costs, and when it ships
The feature description, the design mockup, and the launch date
